'The last will and testament of John Neilson Robertson was proved and registered before the High Court of Justice.

Robertson, pictured at Forest in 1971, also turned out for Scotland and Derby County

After his retirement Robertson went into coaching, holding assistant roles at clubs including Celtic and Aston Villa

'The application has stated that the gross value of the estate in the United Kingdom amounts to £176,250 and the net value amounted to £168,348.'

Robertson made his debut for the Midlands outfit in October 1970 and stayed for a 13-year stint at the club.

He then dropped down into the Second and Third Division with Derby County for a couple of years before returning to end his career at Forest in the top flight in 1985-86.

The left winger made 502 appearances during his two spells at Forest and scored 95 goals.

With Scotland, he is remembered for scoring the winning penalty as they beat England 1-0 at Wembley in 1981 to win the British Home Championship.

He scored against New Zealand in the 1982 World Cup, having also played in the 1978 tournament.

After retiring, he went into coaching and was an assistant to Martin O'Neill at Celtic, and also held an assistant role at Wycombe, Norwich, Leicester and Aston Villa.



In 2015, a poll by the Nottingham Post revealed him to be the fans' all-time favourite player.

Sports.title: Is THIS why Paul Scholes is gunning for old team-mate Michael Carrick? Man United legend's historic comments about disloyalty of current manager - who he calls 'c**p' - emerge
Sports.content:
Is THIS why Paul Scholes is gunning for old team-mate Michael Carrick? Man United legend's historic comments about disloyalty of current manager - who he calls 'c**p' - emerge

Historic comments from Paul Scholes about former team-mate Michael Carrick have emerged after the Manchester United legend took aim at the current Red Devils boss on social media.

Scholes turned on his old friend on Wednesday night, calling United 'c**p' under his leadership after his first defeat in eight games by Newcastle.

In a now-deleted Instagram story, Scholes posted overnight: 'Michael has definitely got something special about him... cos United have been c**p last four games... night'.

Scholes added a kissing face emoji, before writing 'Tonali' and following the Newcastle star's name with a love heart.

The midfielders played together 160 times for Manchester United and England, but Scholes first criticised the current Red Devils boss in 2021 when he suggested he had been disloyal to former manager Ole Gunnar Solskjaer.

Carrick was a first team coach at United under Solskjaer between 2019 and 2021, before the Norwegian was sacked following a poor run of results. Carrick stayed on and took over as interim boss then too, but that was the wrong move, according to Scholes.

Historic comments from Paul Scholes (left) about former team-mate Michael Carrick (right) have emerged on the back of his latest criticism

The former United midfielder said United have been 'c**p last four games' under his old team-mate after the loss

He said at the time: 'I feel with Ole he put a lot of trust into Michael, Kieran (McKenna) and Mike Phelan which is why they shouldn't be at the club now.

'They're all good friends and they're good people but if I was them I'd feel guilty still working at the club after Ole put so much trust in them.'

When quizzed on the comments at the time, Carrick responded: 'It's been an emotional few days, the first person I spoke to after Ed (Woodward) offered me the role was Ole.

'I thought that was the right thing to do.'

During Carrick's current run as interim, Scholes has been coy on if the club should give the 44-year-old the job permanently, suggesting Carlo Ancelotti is in fact the 'perfect' option.

United are set to appoint a long-term boss in the summer after Carrick took over from Ruben Amorim as interim earlier this year, and the former midfielder put his name in the running with an impressive start to his tenure.

He had enjoyed the joint-best record of any manager in Premier League history over their first seven games, winning six of those matches and drawing one - but his run came to an end with the Newcastle defeat.

As players, Carrick and Scholes won five Premier League titles, the Champions League and a FIFA Club World Cup trophy together.

The duo played together 160 times before Scholes questioned Carrick's loyalty when he became United interim manager in 2021

When asked who the best team-mate he's ever had was in 2018, Carrick told The Mirror: 'I always say Scholesy because he was right next to me and, as a partnership, he was fantastic to play with.

'We had some incredible players, amazing, but I always go back to Scholesy.'

But it seems ties may have been cut with Scholes' criticism - and he questioned whether Carrick has the 'experience' to deliver the success United are after in the week.

'The question is, has Michael got the experience?' Scholes said on The Good, The Bad & The Football Podcast. 'I know it's a big question, I know he has managed in the Championship, but this is different.

'Can we see Michael winning the Premier League? I don't know yet, and I think the biggest thing is, what type of manager will they look for? A tactician or a man manager who looks after people?

'Ole Gunnar (Solskjaer) was that. But all the people who are man managers, they always have a brilliant coach next to them, which Michael has got (Steve Holland).



'The club have to decide, do they want a master tactician, which has failed... Ole Gunnar had the man management skills, but did he have the experience to go on and win a trophy? The same questions will be asked of Michael.'

Sports.title: F1 is back so make sure you don't miss a thing with our free enhanced coverage
Sports.content:
F1 is back so make sure you don't miss a thing with our free enhanced coverage

Lando Norris is the defending champion, George Russell is the pre-season favourite and Lewis Hamilton is back in the groove, and there are still so many other gripping storylines besides

View Image F1 is back for the 2026 season (Image: Getty Images)

It’s finally here! The new Formula 1 season is upon us with the lights set to go out for the Australian Grand Prix on Sunday, March 8. And what a year we have in store, with endless storylines to follow in this new era for the sport.

‌



Forget what you think you know about F1. Sweeping new regulations from car design to engine power and so much more have changed the game. Lando Norris is looking to build a dynasty after becoming champion for the first time in 2025 but, with these new rules, anything can happen and he has plenty of rivals who will try to steal his crown.

‌



Our FREE Pit Lane Chronicle Formula 1 newsletter is the perfect way to keep you up to date. No clickbait, no ads and no faff – just the stories and content you need to know about, delivered directly to your inbox and those of the thousands of other fans who are already subscribed.

‌



What is Pit Lane Chronicle about?

Put together by our senior Formula 1 correspondent, Daniel Moxon, Pit Lane Chronicle is a weekly publication sent straight to your inbox with the latest news and interesting content, with dedicated sections for news about the teams who don't always get their fair share of the spotlight.

So if you regularly struggle to wade through the endless tide of content, with dozens of websites publishing the same stories about the likes of Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappen and making it harder for you to find more nuanced reporting, then this newsletter is perfect for you. The best part is it's completely free AND with no adverts.

‌



There are also regular special editions of the newsletter offering insight into some of the biggest stories, as well as exclusive interviews. Examples from last season include this Q&A with 1996 world champion Damon Hill from November, as the title race was approaching its climax, and this sit-down with former Ferrari racer and 11-time F1 race-winner Rubens Barrichello.

Dan said: "I've always felt that teams outside F1's 'big four' don't get the coverage that they, and their fans, deserve. It's understandable in a way – we've all got to deliver for our audiences and there is generally more interest in the bigger names – but it means those who do support midfield or backmarker teams, or their drivers, are getting short-changed.

"That's why I devote a good chunk of my time and effort to this newsletter. I want fans of the seven teams who don't get as much coverage as the likes of Mercedes, McLaren, Ferrari and Red Bull to be able to quickly and easily find quality content about the drivers and teams they love."

Article continues below

Where and how do I sign up?

Pit Lane Chronicle is hosted on Substack, an independent platform with no ads. You can read on your computer or tablet, while there is also a handy app which you can download to your device where you can read each edition. You can 

And, by subscribing, you're making sure you don't have to spend any time seeking us out. Each edition of Pit Lane Chronicle will be delivered directly to your inbox for you to read at your leisure.

Sports.title: Cheltenham 2026: Willie Mullins talent compared to Faugheen has big race target decided
Sports.content:
Cheltenham 2026: Willie Mullins talent compared to Faugheen has big race target decided

The JP McManus-owned Mighty Park has been cut to 7-2 second favourite for the Supreme Novices’ Hurdle after he was removed from the field for the Turners

View 2 Images Mighty Park: new second favourite for the Supreme (Image: RACINGFOTOS.COM)

One of the most talked about horses at the Cheltenham Festival has had his big race target decided by Willie Mullins. The once-raced Mighty Park, owned by billionaire JP McManus, recorded a handsome victory on his hurdling debut by beating 15 rivals by 38 lengths.

‌



He was ante-post favourite for the Turners’ Novices’ Hurdle, Wednesday’s opening race at the Festival, after Mullins sparked a surge of bets when comparing him to the legendary Faugheen last month. He said: “When Mark Walsh got off him after he won at Fairyhouse, he said he looked at the big screen at the second last and couldn’t believe how far he was in front, having gone what he thought was a normal slow pace.

‌



ITV and Cheltenham Festival star stranded in Dubai reveals terrifying explosion nearby READ MORE :

“Mark said his cruising speed must be huge and he’s a fantastic jumper. I think he must be right in the mix for the Supreme. He has to be a hell of a horse to do what he did and has huge natural ability.

‌



“Winning by 38 lengths like he did the other day puts you into Faugheen-type territory. From day one I thought he was good enough to win a Champion Bumper with what he was showing me at home.”

Despite his market prominence Mighty Park was not among the confirmations for the Turners on Thursday after being taking out by his trainer and is now expected to take up his Supreme engagement, a race for which he is 7-2 second favourite with William Hill from 8-1.

‌



View 2 Images Faugheen: winning the 2015 Champion Hurdle (Image: PA)

Harry Fry’s leading Supreme contender Idaho Sun was ruled out of the Cheltenham Festival on Thursday with a setback.

The six-year-old, who was sixth in last year’s Champion Bumper, is unbeaten in three starts since turning his attention to hurdling and was a 12-1 chance for the meeting’s curtain-raiser after landing Grade 1 honours at Aintree on Boxing Day.

Article continues below

However, he will now miss the race, with his absence a blow to the British challenge which is headed by Nicky Henderson’s big-race favourite Old Park Star.

In a post on his X account, Fry said: “Idaho Sun will unfortunately miss the Supreme Novices’ Hurdle at Cheltenham next week due to a hind limb lameness that requires further diagnosis”

Sports.title: F1 races 'under cancellation threat' as deadline for decision revealed
Sports.content:
F1 races 'under cancellation threat' as deadline for decision revealed

As the conflict between the USA, Israel and Iran continues to destabilise the Middle East, F1 appear to be putting in some contingency plans

View 3 Images F1 and the FIA may have some big decisions to make in the next couple of weeks (Image: NurPhoto, NurPhoto via Getty Images)

F1 bosses may have as little as two weeks to make a decision on cancelling the Bahrain Grand Prix and the Saudi Arabian Grand Prix. The two races are at risk due to the US-Israel conflict with Iran.

‌



Air strikes and missiles have been fired in recent weeks as tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, with ramifications affecting the sporting world as a consequence. Doubts had been raised over the Australian Grand Prix, F1's season-opener, but the race appears set to proceed with drivers and personnel in Melbourne.

‌



Not long before, the Pirelli wet tyre testing event in Bahrain had to be cancelled after the Bahraini capital Manama was targeted by an Iranian strike. While the Australian GP looks set to go ahead as planned, the upcoming races in both Bahrain and Saudi may be impacted, depending on the extent of damage in neighbouring regions.

‌



The BBC reports both races are in danger of being axed as F1 and the FIA continue to assess the situation. Bahrain hosts F1's fourth race of the season across the April 10-12 weekend, with Saudi Arabia the location for the following Grand Prix a week afterwards.

A recent statement from F1 said: "Our next three races are in Australia, China and Japan not in the Middle East - those races are not for a number of weeks.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"As always we closely monitor any situation like this and work closely with relevant authorities and will always prioritise the safety and security of all those in the sport."

Nevertheless, F1 must make a decision in the coming fortnight on whether races in Bahrain and Saudi will be scrapped, as equipment needs to be shipped to the two nations in advance. If the two races are cancelled, it is improbable they will be rescheduled for the 2026 season, with the calendar packed and no clear space to accommodate two additional events.

View 3 Images The Pirelli wet tyre testing event in Bahrain was cancelled after an Iranian strike hit the capital city of Manama (Image: Maxar, DigitalGlobe/Getty Images)

‌



Several other locations have been proposed as a substitute this spring, including Portugal, Italy or Turkey. However, it's believed there isn't sufficient time to arrange a Grand Prix at such short notice.

If the conflict is still ongoing when races are due to occur in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia, it's considered unlikely that they will proceed. The ATP organisation in tennis has had to cancel a challenger event in Abu Dhabi when a missile was intercepted nearby, with players evacuated from the Middle East by the governing body.

View 3 Images The Bahrain Grand Prix and Saudi Arabia Grand Prix take place in April (Image: Getty)

‌



Looking ahead to the future races in the Middle East, FIA president Mohammed Ben Sulayem said: "As president of the FIA, my thoughts are with all those affected by the recent events in the Middle East. We are deeply saddened by the loss of life and stand with the families and communities impacted.

"At this moment of uncertainty, we hope for calm, safety, and a swift return to stability. Dialogue and the protection of civilians must remain priorities.

Formula One 2026: The Ultimate Season Preview – New Rules, New Rivals, New Champion From radical new rules to a new world champion in McLaren’s Lando Norris, the 2026 Formula 1 season promises more drama, surprises, and spectacle than ever before. Don’t miss our essential special edition: your definitive guide to the new era of F1. Buy now

Article continues below

"We are in close contact with our Member Clubs, championship promoters, teams, and colleagues on the ground as we monitor developments carefully and responsibly.

"Safety and well-being will guide our decisions as we assess the forthcoming events scheduled there for the FIA World Endurance Championship and the FIA Formula One World Championship. Our organisation is built on unity and shared purpose. That unity matters now more than ever."

Sports.title: Emma Raducanu explains new approach after burning through NINE coaches
Sports.content:
Emma Raducanu explains new approach after burning through NINE coaches

Emma Raducanu is currently without a full-time coach after parting company with Francisco Roig in January and the 23-year-old Brit is now taking a different approach

View 2 Images Emma Raducanu has worked with a lot of coaches (Image: Robert Prange/Getty Images)

Emma Raducanu says she is no rush to find a new long-term coach after splitting with yet another earlier this year. Raducanu parted company with Francisco Roig after the Australian Open and it now taking a different approach to coaching.

‌



Roig was the ninth coach in total and seventh full-time official coach to be axed by Raducanu during her career. The 2021 US Open champion opted to wave goodbye to Roig, who previously worked with Rafael Nadal for 18 years, due to a different of opinions on her playing style.

‌



Since January, Raducanu has been working with her hitting partner Alexis Canter and is happy with the current set-up ahead of Indian Wells in California, where she faces Anastasia Zakharova in the first round.

‌



The 23-year-old has previously worked with Nigel Sears, Andrew Richardson, Torben Beltz, Dmitry Tursunov, Sebastian Sachs, Nick Cavaday, Vladimir Platenik, Mark Petchey and Roig. The world no.24 is still working with Petchey on a day-to-day basis, but the 55-year-old isn't available full-time due to his commitments as a broadcaster with the Tennis Channel.

"Right now, it's more about bringing my instincts back out, getting back in touch with myself," Raducanu told BBC Sport in California. "I have had a lot of people telling me what to do, how to play, and it hasn't necessarily fit.

"So I want to come back to my natural way of playing. That takes time to relearn because that's something that has been coached out of me a little bit.

‌



"I don't necessarily want to have one coach in the role because anyone I bring in is straight away going to be scrutinised, even if it's a trial. I might feel the pressure to stick with them, even if it's not necessarily the right decision.

"I would love to have a coach that works well, but I don't think it's necessarily going to be easy to find one person and they are going to check every box.

View 2 Images Emma Raducanu is ranked 24th in the world - her highest position since 2022 (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

‌



"I definitely have my mind open to it. It's just that I would rather someone not come in and tell me 'let's do this', and I disagree with it but have to listen to them. So far Alexis has been really good, but I am definitely going to tap into a few people here and there."

Raducanu spoke glowingly of Petchey's influence after working with him at Wimbledon last summer. "I'd give him 11 out of 10," she said after beating Mimi Xu in the first round at the All England Club.

"He's been everything the last few months for me. I've really enjoyed being around him. He's helped me so much on all fronts, tennis, off court. I think the most memorable moment... We've had quite a few. There was one hike we did in L.A. We walked for two and a half hours. We spoke.

Article continues below

"It was one of those really, I guess, deep conversations where I was like, okay, certain things I need to do better. It was just one that I could take away and really reflect on."

Sports.title: Jody Townend prepared for ‘plenty of stick’ if she can beat brother Paul at Cheltenham
Sports.content:
Jody Townend prepared for ‘plenty of stick’ if she can beat brother Paul at Cheltenham

The younger sister of Willie Mullins’s stable jockey is looking forward to defending her crown in the Champion Bumper - once she knows what she is riding

View 3 Images Jody Townend: sister of Paul Townend will be hoping to add to her Cheltenham Festival score (Image: PA)

Jody Townend is preparing to take ‘plenty of stick’ if she can beat her brother Paul again at the Cheltenham Festival. Paul Townend, stable jockey to Willie Mullins, has finished top jockey at five of the last six Festivals. His 38 victories are more than double the total of any other jockey competing at the meeting.

‌



But last year another member of the family, his younger sister Jody, joined the roll of honour when she partnered Bambino Fever to victory in the Weatherbys Champion Bumper. The Mullins-trained mare was her only ride of the week but she seized her opportunity to steer the 4-1 chance to a one and a half length success.

‌



ITV and Cheltenham Festival star stranded in Dubai reveals terrifying explosion nearby READ MORE :

Jody, 28, from Midleton in Cork, has recorded a Grade 2 win over hurdles but particularly excels in bumper races. She has seven graded wins to her name, all for Willie Mullins and often when riding the stable’s second or third string.

‌



Mullins has half a dozen entered in this year’s bumper, including three of the first four in the betting, Love Sign d’Aunou, The Irish Avatar and Quiryn, but Jody won’t know which horse she will ride until Patrick Mullins and brother Paul have picked their mounts.

“Nothing's been set in stone,” she says, speaking as an ambassador for Horse.bet . “I haven't been told either way, but last year it was Monday when I checked declarations. I was told I was on Bambino Fever, so I'm not going to be pushing anyone because that didn't work out too bad.”

View 3 Images Jody Townend celebrates after winning the Weatherbys Champion Bumper with Bambino Fever (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Paul Townend finished 12th in last year’s bumper but didn’t begrudge his sister, who rides as an amateur but works full-time at Mullins’s yard, experiencing the unique Cheltenham Festival-winning atmosphere

“He was delighted,” she says. “I gained plenty of stick that I did beat him now. My family were there and he came out for the pictures after and all, so he wasn't too sour.”

She goes on: “I couldn't even imagine what it must be like walking into the winner's enclosure. Being the last race in the day the crowd was smaller, but the atmosphere there was unbelievable, even walking back down the chute.”

‌



View 3 Images Paul Townend: the most successful jockey riding at the Cheltenham Festival (Image: PA)

Being on the second or third string, Jody doesn’t feel the pressure others might when riding a hot favourite at Cheltenham.

“That tends to give me a bit of a free rein to do what I think is right, and, thankfully, it works quite a bit. Riding for Willie you have a chance, no matter what price it is. The man seems to be a genius at training winners and it's kind of a pleasure to be going out, riding for him and to not have the pressure.”

Article continues below

Jody passed up the chance to turn professional six years ago. “I spoke to Willie quite a bit about it. He wanted me to go apprentice on the Flat and then he changed his mind," she explains.

"He said I would have the opportunity to ride some really good horses by staying amateur, and he was right. I've got some unbelievable opportunities out of it and I don't think I'd have ridden three grade one winners otherwise."

Sports.title: Sir Alex Ferguson's verdict on Harry Maguire tells Michael Carrick everything he needs to know
Sports.content:
Sir Alex Ferguson's verdict on Harry Maguire tells Michael Carrick everything he needs to know

What Sir Alex Ferguson really thinks about Harry Maguire as his Manchester United contract ticks down

View 2 Images Sir Alex Ferguson has followed Harry Maguire's career closely (Image: Getty)

Sir Alex Ferguson has already made his feelings clear on Harry Maguire as Manchester United weigh up whether to hand the defender a new deal. The centre-back's contract expires at the end of the season and while there have been positive noises about him extending his stay at Old Trafford, nothing has been agreed yet.

‌



The 33-year-old has spent a large portion of the season on the sidelines recovering from injury but has become a key player since Michael Carrick was named interim head coach in January.

‌



Maguire has been allowed to speak to other clubs since January, turning down approaches from Brendan Rodgers ’ new Saudi club, Al Qadsiah, and AC Milan this year.

‌



The waters are muddied by the fact it is unknown who will lead the club into next season. Carrick is in the frame after an excellent start to reign as interim boss, although United will have options in the summer, when a number of high-profile managers will be on the market.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Maguire already has one influential supporter at Old Trafford in the shape of Ferguson, who has admired the player since seeing him play for Sheffield United in the FA Youth Cup. The then-teenager suffered a concussion during the match against United's youngsters and had to spend a night in hospital.

‌



However, his mood was lifted by a surprise phone call from Ferguson. During their conversation, it's claimed that the legendary boss told Maguire he had what it takes to make it to the top, while also checking on his condition. He also said they'd get to know each other a lot more in the years to come.

Harry Maguire verdict has been reached by Michael Carrick with Man Utd to make decision READ MORE :

Newcastle post cheeky Man Utd dig as match-winner comes back to haunt Michael Carrick READ MORE :

In 2018, five years after his managerial retirement, Ferguson again made his thoughts clear again as United lobbied to sign Maguire following his outstanding displays at the World Cup, where England reached the semi-finals.

The Scot was asked about the battle to sign him at the time. According to the Manchester Evening News , he told a confidant: "They should take the lad in the boardroom and not let him out until he's signed!"

‌



View 2 Images Maguire's United future remains uncertain (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Recently, Maguire has had other issues on his mind after his assault and bribery conviction was upheld six years after he was arrested in Greece. He has been handed a 15-month suspended sentence after a guilty verdict by the Greek courts.

Maguire was charged with non-serious assault, resisting arrest and attempted bribery following an incident on the island of Mykonos in August 2020. After being found guilty on all three counts, Maguire plans to appeal the guilty verdict, having always denied wrongdoing.

Article continues below

He didn't appear in court and played in United's 2-1 defeat at Newcastle on Wednesday night.

Sports.title: Sir Jim Ratcliffe's Man Utd mistake, summoning Michael Carrick for chat and spiky reaction
Sports.content:
Sir Jim Ratcliffe's Man Utd mistake, summoning Michael Carrick for chat and spiky reaction

Another big decision is looming for Sir Jim Ratcliffe as Michael Carrick aims to land the Manchester United job long-term

View 4 Images Michael Carrick suffered his first defeat as interim boss at Newcastle (Image: George Wood/Getty Images)

Sir Jim Ratcliffe was given fresh food for thought as Manchester United's new manager bounce came to a halt against 10-man Newcastle. Michael Carrick saw his unbeaten start to life as interim boss end with a 2-1 loss at St James' Park, where home midfielder Jacob Ramsey collected a second booking for an apparent dive during a dramatic end to the first half.

‌



Despite that, Anthony Gordon fired Newcastle ahead from the spot, only for Casemiro to level even later in stoppage time. United couldn't make their extra tell in the second half as Will Osula's stunning sole effort sealed the points for Eddie Howe's side.

‌



The defeat has not undone Carrick's good work since he took over in the wake of Ruben Amorim's sacking and he remains a contender to land the job on a permanent basis in the summer.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Ratcliffe's mistake

The joint-owner will be especially cautious over his next appointment after admitting the club got it wrong by handing Erik ten Hag a one-year extension. The Dutchman was at the helm when the INEOS billionaire bought into the club.

Harry Maguire verdict has been reached by Michael Carrick with Man Utd to make decision READ MORE :

Newcastle post cheeky Man Utd dig as match-winner comes back to haunt Michael Carrick READ MORE :

Ten Hag oversaw a largely wretched 2023/24 campaign, saved by the FA Cup final victory over Manchester City. That helped him land a new deal, only for him to be sacked the following October and replacing him with Amorim.

‌



Ratcliffe subsequently admitted that keeping Ten Hag on after the FA Cup win and hiring Dan Ashworth as sporting director were errors of judgment. He told Sky Sports last year: "I agree the Erik ten Tag and Dan Ashworth decisions were errors. I think there were some mitigating circumstances, but ultimately they were errors. I accept that and I apologise for that."

View 4 Images Ratcliffe admitted he got it wrong by extending Erik ten Hag's contract (Image: Manchester United via Getty Images)

Carrick summoned

Ratcliffe has clearly been left impressed by Carrick's stewardship of a side that lacked direction under Amorim. After January's win at leaders Arsenal, Carrick delayed his post-match media duties to have a private chat with Ratcliffe.

‌



Asked later about the exchange with the United co-owner, Carrick told Viaplay: "No, not much as you would expect, I think. There's a lot of happy bodies around the camp today and he's delighted as well. So it was nice to see him."

The exchange suggested there was already a good rapport between the manager and his boss.

View 4 Images Carrick has impressed Ratcliffe (Image: Marc Atkins/Getty Images)

‌



Spiky interview

Understandably, Carrick wasn't in a jovial mood following the defeat at Newcastle. But his mood appeared to darken further when it was put to him that his side had lacked character by TNT Sports reporter Jules Breach.

She asked: "You've shown so much character in your games, why was that lacking?" Carrick swiftly responded: "It wasn't. It wasn't." An uncomfortable silence ensued as Breach could be heard saying: "Okay."

View 4 Images Carrick bristled at questions following the defeat at Newcastle (Image: TNT Sports)

‌



Carrick went on: "It's easy to feel that just because you don't win a game of football. Just general things, the quality."

The former midfielder gave another prickly answer when pressed to clarify what was missing from the display. He said: "We've lost one game, alright? We haven't played well enough, but in the grand scheme of things, we're in a decent position."
